items.title: Conte wordt gek van blessures: 'Dit is een absurd seizoen'
items.summary: Antonio Conte is enorm opgelucht na de zege van Napoli bij Genoa, maar heeft ook alarm geslagen. Zijn selectie is wel erg dun geworden. Met de blessure van Scott McTominay nam de schade zaterdagavond verder toe.

items.title: VI Live: Twente walst via gebroeders Rots over Heerenveen heen
items.summary: Zaterdag staan er vier Eredivisie-wedstrijden op het programma, maar ook in het buitenland wordt gevoetbald. Onder meer Manchester United Arsenal, Barcelona, Borussia Dortmund en Napoli komen in actie. Volg al het voetbal in de VI Live.

items.title: Outtara scores late to give Brentford win over Newcastle
items.summary: Dango Outtara scores in the 85th minute to give Brentford a 3-2 win over Newcastle United, as the visitors win their first game at St James' Park since 1934.

items.title: Everton produce second-half comeback to beat Fulham
items.summary: Everton score twice in the final fifteen minutes to secure a 2-1 away win over Fulham at Craven Cottage in the Premier League.

items.title: Gyokeres scores twice as Arsenal beat Sunderland
items.summary: Viktor Gyokeres scores two goals as Arsenal comfortably beat Sunderland 3-0 to extend their lead at the top of the Premier League table to nine points.

items.title: Romero red helps Man Utd continue perfect start under Carrick
items.summary: Goals from Bryan Mbeumo and Bruno Fernandes help Manchester United to a 2-0 win over 10-man Tottenham, who saw captain Cristian Romero sent off for the second time this season in the Premier League.

items.title: Palmer scores first-half hat-trick as Chelsea beat Wolves
items.summary: Cole Palmer becomes the first player in Premier League history to score three career first-half hat-tricks, as Chelsea see off Wolves 3-1 at Molineux.

items.title: Liverpool v City is no longer the Premier League’s big show: how have the mighty fallen? | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has led the way with his tactics for a decade but he has changed course and Arsenal have taken advantage Great rivalries are always more about feel than about numbers. There have been only four Premier League seasons in which Manchester City and Liverpool have finished in the top two positions in the table (and one of those occasions was 2013-14 when the managers were Manuel Pellegrini and Brendan Rodgers, which is not a duel anybody is writing books or making documentaries about). Yet for most of the decade that Pep Guardiola has been at City, it has felt that English football was defined by his struggle with Jürgen Klopp and Liverpool, and by a form of the game that developed as each learned from the other. Continue reading...

items.title: Dango Ouattara tips seesaw contest Brentford’s way to sink Newcastle
items.summary: What price Keith Andrews for manager of the season? Thomas Frank’s surprise successor certainly added to his fanclub as he choreographed Brentford’s fourth win in six Premier League games to leave Eddie Howe looking even gloomier than the unremittingly wet Tyneside weather. While outstanding performances from Dango Ouattara and Keane Lewis-Potter left Andrews’s seventh place side appearing genuine European contenders, Howe’s Newcastle have won only one of their last eight matches in all competitions and lost four of their past five. Continue reading...

items.title: Afgekeurde goal voor de eeuwigheid én late treffer dompelen PEC in rouw
items.summary: Volendam heeft zaterdag in de blessuretijd een belangrijke zege geboekt op bezoek bij PEC Zwolle. Lang leek het duel af te stevenen op een gelijkspel, mede omdat Jadiel Pereira da Gama een historisch doelpunt door zijn neus geboord werd. Brandley Kuwas werd in de slotfase de matchwinner.

items.title: Barça teen Bernal celebrates 'dream' first goal
items.summary: Marc Bernal celebrated a dream moment as the midfielder stepped up his recovery from an ACL injury by scoring his first Barcelona goal in Saturday's 3-0 LaLiga win over Mallorca.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal's 9-point lead means 'nothing'
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has claimed Arsenal's nine-point lead at the top of the Premier League means "nothing" despite his team piling the pressure on Manchester City.

items.title: Carrick: Utd haven't 'cracked it' after Spurs win
items.summary: Michael Carrick is not being fooled into thinking Manchester United have "cracked it" after his dream start continued by beating a Tottenham side that captain Cristian Romero apologised to after his costly red card.

items.title: Wrexham suffer double injury blow in home loss
items.summary: Wrexham suffered a double injury blow in Saturday's 2-0 home loss to Millwall to compound a miserable day for the Championship club as celebrity co-owner Ryan Reynolds watched on in north Wales.

items.title: Goal van jubilaris Botman wordt overschaduwd: Newcastle verliest weer
items.summary: Sven Botman heeft in zijn honderdste wedstrijd voor Newcastle United gescoord, maar zal toch flink balen na de ontmoeting met Brentford. The Magpies gingen zaterdagavond voor de derde keer op rij onderuit in de Premier League.

items.title: Klopjacht op doldwaze eekhoorn zorgt voor minutenlang oponthoud in Championship
items.summary: Waar Hull City en Bristol City zaterdagmiddag de jacht op drie punten openden, hadden de aanwezige beveiligers vooral hun pijlen op een opmerkelijke veldbestormer gericht. Het Championship-duel werd tot twee keer toe tijdelijk stilgelegd door een doldwaze eekhoorn. Het knaagdier rende het hele veld rond en zorgden voor gelach én gejuich op de tribunes.

items.title: Four in a row for 'special' Carrick - have Man Utd finally transformed?
items.summary: It's four Premier League wins in a row for Manchester United under Michael Carrick and a season that was unraveling just weeks ago now looks full of potential and promise.

items.title: Newcastle latest: Where does Brentford defeat leave Howe? Have your say!
Suryakumar rescues India after USA scare at T20 World Cup
items.summary: Suryakumar Yadav spared India's blushes with a superb unbeaten 84 from 49 balls as the co-hosts beat USA by 29 runs in their opening T20 World Cup match in Mumbai.

items.title: Gyokeres' new role, reckless Romero and Carrick cracking it at Man Utd
